title: Phlomoides muliensis
taxonomy: Phlomoides muliensis belongs to the family Lamiaceae, which is commonly known as the mint family. It is a species of flowering plant within the genus Phlomoides. Phlomoides muliensis is native to China, specifically found in the Sichuan and Yunnan provinces. The plant is classified as an annual herb, characterized by its small, white, or pink flowers. Further classification may involve specific subgroups, but its primary classification is within the Lamiaceae family.

intro: Phlomoides muliensis, also known as Sichuan calamint, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of Sichuan province in China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Phlomoides muliensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being added to traditional Chinese medicine formulas for its therapeutic benefits.
